target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, trim entries, drop blank/whitespace-only entries, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Normalize Gateway `agent.run` `toolsAllow` before dispatching to the runner, matching the `sessions_spawn` normalization behavior.
- Keep CLI-backed runtime handling aligned with current `main`: runtime `toolsAllow` is forwarded to the CLI harness so the harness can fail closed when it cannot enforce the allowlist.

## Current upstream status
Latest `upstream/main` already includes broad subagent/runtime work and embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support. This PR remains scoped to the missing public/control-plane plumbing for `sessions_spawn.toolsAllow`, plus Gateway normalization for raw `agent.run` inputs.

## Change Type
- [x] Feature

## Scope
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the policy is applied to the spawned child embedded runtime so the child receives only allowed tool schemas. Gateway `agent.run` also normalizes direct `toolsAllow` input before runner dispatch.

Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout at current PR head `8ffac4d1fc59032d23f7a97a2bf1a03752fbb3e6`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, local Gateway on loopback token auth, `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json` through Gateway (not `--local`), and a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock server that records every `/v1/responses` request tool schema.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started a local mock OpenAI Responses server on `127.0.0.1:19245`, started OpenClaw Gateway on `127.0.0.1:19246`, then ran the parent agent through Gateway:

```text
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.kEqyfu/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.kEqyfu/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=proof-token-78441 OPENAI_API_KEY=mock node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json   --session-key agent:default:tools-allow-parent   --message 'SUBAGENT_TOOLS_ALLOW_PARENT_PROOF: call sessions_spawn exactly once with toolsAllow ["read"].'   --timeout 90
```

The mock model returned a real `sessions_spawn` function call from the parent request with `toolsAllow:["read"]`; the Gateway run accepted it, spawned the child, and the child model request was logged from the same mock provider.

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the current-head Gateway proof summarizer. The parent request had `sessions_spawn`; the spawned child request had only `read`, with `exec`, `sessions_spawn`, and `sessions_yield` absent.

```json
{
  "head": "8ffac4d1fc59032d23f7a97a2bf1a03752fbb3e6",
  "gatewayAgentRunStatus": "ok",
  "gatewayAgentRunId": "ba926aed-4c88-41bf-b00e-166c45c58f5a",
  "finalAssistantVisibleText": "CHILD_DONE",
  "parentTools": [
    "exec",
    "read",
    "sessions_spawn",
    "sessions_yield",
    "subagents"
  ],
  "restrictedChildTools": [
    "read"
  ],
  "restrictedChildHasRead": true,
  "restrictedChildHasExec": false,
  "restrictedChildHasSessionsSpawn": false,
  "restrictedChildHasSessionsYield": false,
  "proofDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.kEqyfu"
}
```

Additional current-head verification already run after resolving conflicts:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts --testNamePattern toolsAllow --reporter=dot
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  8 passed | 300 skipped (308)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts --testNamePattern 'toolsAllow|timestamp context|canonical Anthropic' --reporter=dot
Test Files  8 passed (8)
Tests  30 passed | 488 skipped (518)

node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.
```

Observed result after fix: A real Gateway-backed parent agent run called `sessions_spawn` with `toolsAllow:["read"]`, the child run completed with `CHILD_DONE`, and the child model request only advertised `read`. Disallowed parent/session/runtime tools were not present in the child request.

What was not tested: A paid external provider was not used. The proof used a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock server to avoid provider cost while exercising the real Gateway, real embedded runner, real `sessions_spawn` tool call, and real child model request construction.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`.
- Scenario locked in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported ACP runtime, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, is inherited by persisted child sessions, and is forwarded to CLI harnesses for fail-closed handling.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. `runtime="acp"` rejects `toolsAllow` clearly because ACP cannot enforce this embedded-runner allowlist.

## Security Impact
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

Risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native subagent runs. Unsupported ACP paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced. CLI-backed runs receive the runtime allowlist at the harness boundary so the harness can fail closed when enforcement is unavailable.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect all CLI harnesses to enforce runtime allowlists.
  - Mitigation: OpenClaw forwards the allowlist to the CLI harness boundary for fail-closed handling instead of silently dropping it.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.body:
## Summary

- Adds additive `agentRuntime` metadata to `models.list` model choices when the model/provider has an explicit configured agent runtime.
- Shows non-default runtime labels in the WebUI model picker (for example `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex`) without changing the canonical model value (`openai/gpt-5.5`).
- Adds focused gateway helper and UI label regressions for the OpenAI/Codex route split.

Refs #90420 (real 6.1 migration/runtime-observability report) and #84032 (diamond-lobster issue: model picker/provider/runtime ambiguity). Related to #90036 and #90047 because the live Windows 6.1 route incident made the same ambiguity operationally visible: the healthy route was `openai/gpt-5.5` + Codex runtime, while stale durable state still surfaced as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.

## Why

After the 2026.6.1 Codex migration, a healthy route can be:

```text
model ref: openai/gpt-5.5
runtime: codex
```

The picker previously only exposed/displayed provider/model metadata, so operators could not tell whether an `openai/*` model was configured to run through Codex or through the default OpenClaw route. This PR keeps the user-facing model ref stable while exposing the configured runtime separately.

## Verification

target.title: fix(sessions): append new entry when file exists in persist() no-assistant guard (fixes #89206)
target.body:
## Summary

Fix the session persist path so resuming a `header + user-only` file no longer triggers a full in-memory buffer rewrite on the next assistant entry. The change is a narrow guard in `persist()` that appends the new entry immediately when the file already exists, keeping the `flushed` invariant true.

**Scope**: 1 source file (`session-manager.ts`), 1 test file
**Changes**: `src/agents/sessions/session-manager.ts` (+10/-2), `session-manager.test.ts` (+73)

## Root Cause

`persist()` has a `no-assistant` guard that sets `flushed = false` for all call paths, deferring the first disk write until an assistant entry arrives. Prior commit 2c48dd2277 swapped `appendJsonlEntriesSync` for `writeJsonlEntriesSync` in the `!flushed` branch to avoid appending a duplicate header, but the root cause — the unnecessary reset of the flushed invariant — remained.

When `setSessionFile` loads an existing file (resume / corrupt-header recovery), the file already contains a session header on disk. Resetting `flushed = false` on the next user-only turn means the assistant-triggered `persist()` will rewrite the entire in-memory buffer (header included) over the existing file. The overwrite is safe with `writeFileSync` today but is fragile: if the write strategy changes back to append for performance, the duplicate-header bug returns.

The fix checks `existsSync(this.sessionFile)`: when the file already exists, the header is already on disk so we can safely `appendJsonlEntrySync` the new entry immediately and keep `flushed = true`. Only when the file does not exist (newSession path) do we defer to the assistant arrival so the header is written first.

### Violated invariant

`flushed` tracks whether the on-disk session file matches `fileEntries`. The `no-assistant` guard breaks this invariant when the file already exists on disk by marking it out of sync even though the only change is a new entry that could have been appended.

### Code path

`setSessionFile()` (resume/recovery) → `flushed = true` →
`appendMessage(user)` → `appendEntry()` → `persist()` →
`!hasAssistant` → `flushed = false` (BUG: file already on disk) →
`appendMessage(assistant)` → `!flushed` → `writeJsonlEntriesSync(fullBuffer)`

## Verification

```
pnpm build  # passed
pnpm test src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts  # 3 tests passed
pnpm test src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts src/agents/sessions/auth-storage.test.ts src/agents/sessions/sdk.test.ts  # 11 tests passed (3 files)
```

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Session persist was rewriting the full in-memory buffer on the next assistant entry after a resume/recovery path, risking duplicate headers if the write strategy changed. Now new entries are appended immediately when the file already exists, preserving the flushed invariant.

**Real environment tested**: Ubuntu 24.04, Node v22.22.0, OpenClaw main @ fc6400ede3

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
1. `pnpm build` — project compiles cleanly
2. `pnpm test src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts` — 3 tests pass including the new `"does not duplicate header or user turn when resuming a header+user-only file"` regression test
3. `pnpm test src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts src/agents/sessions/auth-storage.test.ts src/agents/sessions/sdk.test.ts` — 11 tests pass across 3 session test files

**Evidence after fix**:
- Test file: [src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/fix/issue-89206-persist-flush-resume/src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts) — new test at L80–L146 covers Case 2 (resume header+user-only file)
- Source fix: [src/agents/sessions/session-manager.ts](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/fix/issue-89206-persist-flush-resume/src/agents/sessions/session-manager.ts) — `persist()` L904–L916
- Regression test output:
```
Test Files  3 passed (3)
     Tests  11 passed (11)
```

**Observed result after fix**: All 3 session-manager tests pass, including the new regression test for Case 2. The `persist()` method now correctly appends new entries when the file already exists and keeps `flushed = true`, eliminating the need for a full buffer rewrite that could duplicate entries.

**What was not tested**: Long-running session with many user-then-assistant cycles across multiple resume/restart boundaries; concurrent write scenarios (single-process assumption unchanged).

## Review Findings Addressed

N/A — this is a new PR targeting an open issue with no prior review findings.

## Regression Test Plan

- `pnpm test src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts` — session-manager unit tests (3 tests)
- `pnpm test src/agents/sessions/` — full session test suite
- Manual smoke: `openclaw --continue` after a user-only turn in a previous session

## Merge Risk

🔹 **Low**. The change is a narrow guard (8 lines of logic) in a single function. The `existsSync` check is deterministic; the `appendJsonlEntrySync` path mirrors the existing `else` branch at L914. No new dependencies, no API changes, no behavioral changes for callers.

- Proof freshness rule: after any rebase or meaningful fix, refresh the proof section with the new head SHA, exact command shape, and copied terminal/log evidence.
- Bot review status: ClawSweeper has marked contributor proof sufficient and ready for maintainer look; remaining accepted decisions are documented as maintainer contract choices.
- Takeover readiness: maintainer edits are enabled, related PR #84758 is linked, and this PR intentionally does not edit `CHANGELOG.md`.
- Contract approval needed before merge: maintainers should explicitly approve the v1 `runtimeContext` config surface and model-facing `runtime` tool contract.

## Goal

Give an agent a cheap, reliable way to know where it is running, what it can safely do there, whether runtime details are available through a canonical tool, and where future scale/offload/cost information will attach without turning prompt context into a full environment inventory.

Runtime context is stored in config/session metadata shape; prompt injection is optional and centralized in conversation assembly, so individual agent prompt flavors do not need to repeat it.

## Implemented v1 slice

- Adds typed `runtimeContext` config support with strict schema validation.
- Supports exposure modes: `none`, `tool_hint`, and `prompt_summary`.
- Injects runtime self-context through the existing hidden `openclaw.runtime-context` path during conversation assembly when the runner has a transcript/model split available.
- Keeps `none` fully silent: no prompt injection and no runtime tool.
- Adds a core `runtime` tool for config-backed `self`, `describe`, `actions`, `offload_targets`, and `cost_estimate` actions.
- Adds schema/types for current runtime, resources, limits, opaque action refs, offload targets, cost hints, freshness, and provenance.
- Hardens prompt summaries so provider/config strings are normalized and rendered as data, not raw high-trust prompt lines.
- Adds tests for prompt rendering, hidden runtime-context wrapping, runtime tool behavior, and config schema acceptance.
- Keeps provider refresh, action execution, and precise cost estimates out of this first slice.

## Team review findings addressed

- Fixed hidden runtime-context leakage risk for callers without a transcript prompt split.
- Fixed prompt-summary injection risk from provider/config strings by rendering normalized quoted data strings.
- Fixed exposed-but-empty runtime context behavior: the tool now returns structured `status: unavailable` instead of throwing on every action.
- Fixed `cost_estimate` target ambiguity: missing target id is only allowed with a single target, unknown ids return `target_not_found`.
- Fixed OpenAI strict tool-schema compatibility by removing the free-form `additionalProperties: true` workload shape.
- Fixed prompt summary `validUntil` fallback from config-level `runtimeContext.validUntil`.

## Non-goals

- No broad environment inventory in v1.
- No scheduler/load model.
- No capacity slots.
- No CPU/memory class fields.
- No raw provider commands in prompt-facing data.
- No provider-backed runtime refresh yet.
- No action-ref execution yet.
- No precise provider cost estimator yet.

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: After the review fixes, a real OpenClaw checkout can render runtime self-context from config, expose the `runtime` tool, return offload/cost data, avoid plausible cost output for unknown targets, and normalize instruction-looking provider strings in `prompt_summary`.

target.title: fix(feishu): retry on send rate-limit errors (230020/230006)
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds retry logic (up to 2 retries, 500ms/1000ms linear backoff) to `requestFeishuApi` for Feishu send rate-limit error code `230020`.
- Both `im.message.create` (direct send) and `im.message.reply` (reply send) paths now go through retry.
- Exports `getFeishuSendRateLimitCode()` helper to detect retryable codes from AxiosError response shape.
- Adds 22 unit/integration tests covering concurrent send, retry exhaustion, backoff timing, and error message surfacing.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/send.retry.test.ts` — 13 tests pass
- [x] `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/send.concurrent.test.ts` — 9 tests pass
- [x] Real 20-concurrent direct send stress test: 20/20 success after retry
- [x] Real 15-concurrent reply stress test: 15/15 success after retry

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Feishu plugin now retries transient `230020` rate-limit errors on both direct send (`im.message.create`) and reply (`im.message.reply`) paths, instead of failing immediately and losing messages.

**Real environment tested**: Live Feishu API (`open.feishu.cn`), app `cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9`, macOS local OpenClaw dev build with `npx tsx` runner.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
# Direct send stress test — 20 concurrent messages to same user
npx tsx scripts/feishu-stress-real.mts

# Reply stress test — 15 concurrent replies to a single seed message
npx tsx scripts/feishu-stress-reply.mts --count=15
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Direct send (`im.message.create`) — 20 concurrent:
```
[config] appId=cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9  target=ou_33af95b6f9c0e5fbcaccef732f03d720  count=20

Firing 20 concurrent sends through requestFeishuApi (with retry) …
```

target.title: fix(cron): prune isolated-target cron sessions that lack :run: key segment
target.body:
## Summary

The cron session reaper only pruned sessions with the `cron:<jobId>:run:<uuid>` key pattern (main-target jobs). Isolated cron sessions use `cron:<jobId>` keys without the `:run:` segment, so they were never matched by `isCronRunSessionKey` and accumulated indefinitely regardless of the configured `sessionRetention` value.

## Changes

- Add `isCronIsolatedSessionKey()` to match `cron:<jobId>` keys that lack `:run:`.
- Update the `sweepCronRunSessions` loop to prune both main-target run sessions and isolated cron sessions.
- Add a regression test verifying isolated session pruning.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: After the fix, cron session retention only prunes expired canonical `agent:<id>:cron:<jobId>:run:<runId>` run-session descendants and preserves stable `agent:<id>:cron:<jobId>` base rows, avoiding accidental deletion of carried base-session metadata unless a separate maintainer-approved lifecycle cleanup is introduced.
Real environment tested: Repository `openclaw/openclaw`, branch `fix/cron-isolated-session-pruning`, commit `057bba37c605`, refreshed against current `origin/main` in worktree `/datad/github/openclaw-pr89721`; proof used a copied real OpenClaw agent session-store directory under `/root/.openclaw/agents` and production SQLite session-store APIs, with redacted injected cron rows in the copy only.
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```shell
git diff --check
pnpm exec oxlint src/cron/session-reaper.ts src/cron/session-reaper.test.ts
OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cron/session-reaper.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/tsx /tmp/openclaw-89721-real-store-proof.ts | tee /tmp/openclaw-89721-real-store-proof.json
```
Evidence after fix: Verification output:
```text
=== diff --check ===
=== oxlint ===
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 49ms on 2 files with 208 rules using 8 threads.
=== targeted test ===
src/cron/session-reaper.test.ts: 18 tests passed
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 39.49s

Real/copied store proof summary:
{
  "realStoreRoot": "/root/.openclaw/agents",
  "selectedAgent": "head-of-operations",
  "realEntryCountBeforeInjection": 0,
  "injectedBefore": {
    "stableBasePresent": true,
    "activeBasePresent": true,
    "oldRunPresent": true,
    "recentRunPresent": true
  },
  "sweepResult": { "swept": true, "pruned": 1 },
  "injectedAfter": {
    "stableBasePresent": true,
    "activeBasePresent": true,
    "oldRunPresent": false,
    "recentRunPresent": true
  },
  "afterEntryCount": 3,
  "warningCount": 0
}
```
Observed result after fix: The expired run descendant was pruned, the old stable base cron row remained present, the recent run row remained present, and the reaper reported one pruned row with no warnings. This directly verifies the review-requested boundary: base rows are not age-pruned by `cron.sessionRetention`.
What was not tested: Full repository test suite and a live production gateway cron run were not executed. The proof copied a real local session-store directory and injected redacted cron rows into the copy because the currently readable local stores had no pre-existing cron rows to mutate safely.

## Test Plan

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cron/session-reaper.test.ts` — 18 tests pass (existing + 1 new isolated session pruning test).
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/cron/session-reaper.ts src/cron/session-reaper.test.ts` — 0 errors.
- `git diff --check` — clean.

## Risk

Low. The change only extends the reaper to recognize an additional key shape it previously ignored. Base cron sessions (`cron:<jobId>`) are still pruned by the same retention logic; no base session is deleted prematurely.

Closes #89666

target.title: fix(codex): return diagnostic snapshot on app inventory refresh failure
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes sub-problem #2 of #91352: native Codex `codexPlugins` app inventory refresh failures no longer propagate as unhandled rejections, so normal Codex turns can continue without plugin apps instead of failing the harness.

When `app/list` fails in `CodexAppInventoryCache.refreshUncoalesced`, the method now returns a best-effort `CodexAppInventorySnapshot` carrying a `lastError` diagnostic. Callers that already try/catch `refreshNow` (thread config and plugin activation) continue to work, and the cache no longer forces every consumer to handle rejection propagation.

## Changes

- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-inventory-cache.ts`
  - Replace `throw error` in `refreshUncoalesced` with a diagnostic snapshot return.
  - If a previous successful snapshot exists, preserve its apps and attach `lastError`.
  - If no prior snapshot exists, return an empty snapshot with `expiresAtMs: 0` so `read()` still reports `missing` on the next lookup.

- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-inventory-cache.test.ts`
  - Update existing test to assert resolve-with-diagnostic instead of rejection.
  - Add regression test for the first-refresh-failure case (empty cache, no prior entry).

## Verification

```bash
# Focused cache tests
pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-inventory-cache.test.ts
# 8 passed

# Thread config tests that exercise refreshNow through buildCodexPluginThreadConfig
pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/plugin-thread-config.test.ts
# 14 passed

# Plugin inventory tests
pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/plugin-inventory.test.ts
# 5 passed

# Lint
npx oxlint --import-plugin --config .oxlintrc.json   extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-inventory-cache.ts   extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-inventory-cache.test.ts
# clean
```

## Behavior addressed

- Real environment: Codex `app/list` can fail when the app server is unreachable or auth is expired.
- Symptom: `codex app inventory refresh failed` + `codex plugin thread config app inventory refresh failed` logged, then the Codex harness turn could fail closed.
- After fix: the cache layer returns a diagnostic snapshot, thread config emits `app_inventory_missing` / `app_inventory_stale` diagnostics, and the Codex thread continues with plugin apps excluded.

Closes #91352 (sub-problem #2)

target.title: fix(memory-core): exclude archived transcripts from Dreaming session corpus
target.body:
## Summary

Archived session transcripts (`.jsonl.reset.*` and `.jsonl.deleted.*`) are
currently ingested into the Dreaming corpus, polluting analysis with
reset/deleted cron-spawned subagent noise. On a cron-heavy deployment,
this causes 67-91% of the daily corpus to be dominated by automation
noise, suppressing pattern discovery. This is particularly visible on
workspaces where cron jobs frequently spawn subagents whose transcripts get
reset/deleted but remain in the session directory as usage-counted archives.

## Changes

- Reuse the SDK-exported `isSessionArchiveArtifactName` classifier so Dreaming
  follows the canonical archive filename contract instead of carrying a local regex
- Add archive-exclusion filter in `collectSessionIngestionBatches` so
  Dreaming corpus assembly skips archived transcripts
- Preserve `memory_search` archive indexing path unchanged (only Dreaming
  ingestion is affected)
- Add corpus-level regression coverage for fresh ordinary reset archives,
  including archive timestamps without milliseconds (`...T18-10-00Z`)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Before the fix, Dreaming session-corpus assembly could ingest usage-counted reset/deleted archive transcripts and, after refreshing against current main, could also fail to preserve skipped-transcript checkpoint semantics for generated dreaming/isolated-cron transcripts. After the fix, Dreaming excludes session archive artifacts from the session corpus, keeps generated dreaming/cron run transcripts out of corpus output, preserves skipped checkpoints with zero ingested content lines, and keeps legacy JSON `sessions.json` classification compatible with the current SQLite session-store loader.
Real environment tested: Repository `openclaw/openclaw`, branch `fix/dreaming-exclude-archives`, commit `61fd2d6f9cc5`, local Linux worktree `/datad/github/openclaw-issue90313` refreshed against current `origin/main`, using real OpenClaw session storage under `/root/.openclaw/agents` for the patched Dreaming storage proof and a temp workspace/plugin-state store for output isolation.
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```shell
git diff --check
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.ts
OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/tsx /tmp/openclaw-90433-real-storage-proof.ts > /tmp/openclaw-90433-real-storage-proof-clean.json
jq '{realStateRoot,totalJsonl,archiveCount,liveCount,agentCount,corpusFileCount:(.corpusFiles|length),liveLineCount,archiveAbsence: [.archiveAbsence[] | {agent,file,absent}][0:5]}' /tmp/openclaw-90433-real-storage-proof-clean.json
```
Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the verification:
```
=== diff --check ===
=== oxlint changed files ===
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 49ms on 2 files with 208 rules using 8 threads.
=== focused tests quick rerun ===
packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts: 12 passed
extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts: 45 passed
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 112.86s

Real storage proof summary:
{
  "realStateRoot": "/root/.openclaw/agents",
  "totalJsonl": 627,
  "archiveCount": 73,
  "liveCount": 554,
  "agentCount": 7,
  "corpusFileCount": 17,
  "liveLineCount": 240,
  "archiveAbsence": [
    { "agent": "hr-agent", "file": "30d9dd9e-8281-48eb-bf65-c2d4086d7163.jsonl.reset.2026-05-31T02-45-36.108Z", "absent": true },
    { "agent": "hr-agent", "file": "37325983-4da0-49cc-8747-1bdcf4b0342a.jsonl.reset.2026-06-03T08-50-31.129Z", "absent": true },
    { "agent": "hr-agent", "file": "3b72e1cc-a0c0-4378-92fa-473dfe21551a.jsonl.reset.2026-05-23T13-55-57.715Z", "absent": true },
    { "agent": "hr-agent", "file": "43fb8e14-c895-46c0-a5eb-4eae0014cbbd.jsonl.reset.2026-05-29T23-16-37.488Z", "absent": true },
    { "agent": "hr-agent", "file": "53c23768-32e0-4772-9f8a-0944bde75f52.jsonl.reset.2026-05-29T00-41-25.765Z", "absent": true }
  ]
}
```
Observed result after fix: The refreshed branch builds a Dreaming session corpus from live real session storage while sampled `.jsonl.reset.*` archive transcripts remain absent from the corpus; generated dreaming and isolated cron transcript checkpoints are retained as zero ingested content lines; focused regression suites pass on the refreshed main branch.
What was not tested: Full repository test suite and full production gateway run were not executed; validation was limited to changed-file lint, focused memory-core/SDK regressions, and a patched Dreaming run over real local session storage with redacted/archive-only file identifiers.

## Test Plan

1. `git diff --check`
2. `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts` — 0 warnings, 0 errors
3. `OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts` — 45 tests, all passing
4. `OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts` — 12 tests, all passing

## Risk

- Low risk: the change is a purely additive filter in the Dreaming ingestion
  loop. Archive files are still indexed for `memory_search` through the
  unchanged `listSessionFilesForAgent` → `buildSessionEntry` path used by
  `manager-sync-ops.ts` and `qmd-manager.ts`.
- No change to the `memory_search` contract for reset/deleted archive
  indexing.

## Related Issue

Refs #90313 (archive transcript Dreaming corpus pollution).
Note: the broader cron-descended subagent classification issue mentioned in
the linked report is outside this patch's scope and remains tracked separately.

target.title: fix(codex): return diagnostic snapshot on app inventory refresh failure
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes sub-problem #2 of #91352: native Codex `codexPlugins` app inventory refresh failures no longer propagate as unhandled rejections, so normal Codex turns can continue without plugin apps instead of failing the harness.

When `app/list` fails in `CodexAppInventoryCache.refreshUncoalesced`, the method now returns a best-effort `CodexAppInventorySnapshot` carrying a `lastError` diagnostic. Callers that already try/catch `refreshNow` (thread config and plugin activation) continue to work, and the cache no longer forces every consumer to handle rejection propagation.

## Changes

- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-inventory-cache.ts`
  - Replace `throw error` in `refreshUncoalesced` with a diagnostic snapshot return.
  - If a previous successful snapshot exists, preserve its apps and attach `lastError`.
  - If no prior snapshot exists, return an empty snapshot with `expiresAtMs: 0` so `read()` still reports `missing` on the next lookup.

- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-inventory-cache.test.ts`
  - Update existing test to assert resolve-with-diagnostic instead of rejection.
  - Add regression test for the first-refresh-failure case (empty cache, no prior entry).

## Verification

```bash
# Focused cache tests
pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-inventory-cache.test.ts
# 8 passed

# Thread config tests that exercise refreshNow through buildCodexPluginThreadConfig
pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/plugin-thread-config.test.ts
# 14 passed

# Plugin inventory tests
pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/plugin-inventory.test.ts
# 5 passed

# Lint
npx oxlint --import-plugin --config .oxlintrc.json   extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-inventory-cache.ts   extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-inventory-cache.test.ts
# clean
```

## Behavior addressed

- Real environment: Codex `app/list` can fail when the app server is unreachable or auth is expired.
- Symptom: `codex app inventory refresh failed` + `codex plugin thread config app inventory refresh failed` logged, then the Codex harness turn could fail closed.
- After fix: the cache layer returns a diagnostic snapshot, thread config emits `app_inventory_missing` / `app_inventory_stale` diagnostics, and the Codex thread continues with plugin apps excluded.

Closes #91352 (sub-problem #2)

target.title: fix(google): enable vertex image and video generation
target.body:
## Summary

Vertex AI generative video currently experiences SDK integration failures when fetching credentials dynamically during the predictLongRunning and polling lifecycle via `gaxios`. This patch safely unblocks video generation by explicitly identifying `google-vertex` requests and routing them to a robust REST fallback path.

- Registers proper runtime capabilities for the `google-vertex` identifier for both image and video generation providers.
- Adds proper project and location resolution for Vertex AI based on environment variables or config.
- Bypasses the `@google/genai` SDK and explicitly routes video `predictLongRunning` correctly through Vertex regional domains instead of AI Studio.
- Updates polling logic to use the `fetchPredictOperation` POST verb mandated by Vertex AI.
- Adapts generated video response parsing to handle Vertex-specific `videos: [{bytesBase64Encoded: ...}]` payload structure.
- Explicitly handles `google-vertex` path building for image generation endpoint (`generateContent`).
- Preserves the `qa-lab` allowlist check by discarding unrelated test changes.
- Resolves conflict on the main branch properly configuring the `resolveGoogleVertexBaseOrigin` API.
- Reconstructs internal object structures properly so both Typescript and oxlint tools pass on Github Actions CI.

Fixes #90209.

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** When a user generates an image or video using the `google-vertex` provider, the request fails because the capability runtime is missing the `google-vertex` identifier. Even if it were present, the video SDK crashes on Vertex ADC and routes incorrectly through AI Studio (`generativelanguage.googleapis.com`) causing `404 Not Found` errors. After the fix, both Vertex image and video endpoints are successfully targeted using their proper regional AI Platform origins and required polling schema.

**Real environment tested:**
- OS: Linux (WSL2 / Ubuntu 24.04)
- Node.js: v26.0.0
- Commit tested: `2a75106d732c2add6da6c1e2e3b9d266fe9d6e50`
- Local OpenClaw workspace: `/home/nyu/openclaw`

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
1. Clone the fork and check out the PR branch.
2. Ensure you have valid ADC credentials.
3. Set `GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION=us-central1`.
4. Run image generation using the `google-vertex` model.
5. Run video generation using the `google-vertex` model with `OPENCLAW_DEBUG=1` to observe the REST paths.

**Evidence after fix:**

Image generation:
```bash
$ npx tsx src/entry.ts capability image generate --model google-vertex/gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview --prompt "A cute small tuxedo rutin chicken with cyber goggles on an RGB keyboard" --output /tmp/test_image.png

...
image.generate via local
provider: google-vertex
model: gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview
outputs: 1
/tmp/test_image.png

$ file /tmp/test_image.png
/tmp/test_image.png: PNG image data, 1408 x 768, 8-bit/color RGB, non-interlaced
```

Video generation:
```bash
$ GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION=us-central1 OPENCLAW_DEBUG=1 npx tsx src/entry.ts capability video generate --model google-vertex/veo-3.1-fast-generate-001 --prompt "A cute small tuxedo rutin chicken with cyber goggles pecking on an RGB keyboard" --duration 5 --output /tmp/test_video.mp4

...
The project/location from the environment variables will take precedence over the API key from the environment variables.
[DEBUG] requestGoogleVideoJson URL: POST https://us-central1-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/nyu-ai-gemini/locations/us-central1/publishers/google/models/veo-3.1-fast-generate-001:predictLongRunning
[DEBUG] requestGoogleVideoJson URL: POST https://us-central1-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/nyu-ai-gemini/locations/us-central1/publishers/google/models/veo-3.1-fast-generate-001:fetchPredictOperation
[DEBUG] requestGoogleVideoJson URL: POST https://us-central1-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/nyu-ai-gemini/locations/us-central1/publishers/google/models/veo-3.1-fast-generate-001:fetchPredictOperation
[DEBUG] requestGoogleVideoJson URL: POST https://us-central1-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/nyu-ai-gemini/locations/us-central1/publishers/google/models/veo-3.1-fast-generate-001:fetchPredictOperation
video.generate via local
provider: google-vertex
model: veo-3.1-fast-generate-001
outputs: 1
/tmp/test_video.mp4

$ file /tmp/test_video.mp4
/tmp/test_video.mp4: ISO Media, MP4 Base Media v1 [ISO 14496-12:2003]
```

**Observed result after the fix:** The CLI successfully routes both `google-vertex` image and video payloads via `https://<location>-aiplatform.googleapis.com`. The `predictLongRunning` job initiates properly, and the polling mechanism queries using the `fetchPredictOperation` POST endpoint until completion, avoiding the `gaxios` runtime crash and downloading a valid MP4/PNG.

**Not tested:**
- External proxy deployments of this fix.

target.title: fix: recover terminal session status on visible inbound turns
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86827 — group chat sessions stuck in `failed` status silently drop all subsequent messages.

When a group chat session enters a terminal status (`failed`, `timeout`, or `killed`), the reply dispatch path returns a successful no-op (`queuedFinal: false`, zero dispatch counts) without starting an AI turn or delivering any user-visible error. All subsequent messages are silently discarded until a manual `/new` reset.

## Root Cause

Three independent mechanisms conspire to create the silent drop:

1. **Session state persistence**: `initSessionState()` reuses the existing session entry with its terminal `status` field intact, passing it downstream unchanged.
2. **Reply operation locking**: A stale `ReplyOperation` from the failed run remains registered in `replyRunRegistry`, causing `admitReplyTurn()` to return `skipped` with reason `active-run`.
3. **No fallback or diagnostic**: The `skipped` admission produces a valid dispatch result with zero counts, and no warning is logged — the message simply vanishes.

## Changes

### 1. Auto-recover terminal sessions (`session.ts`)

When a **visible** (non-cron/heartbeat) inbound message hits a session with recoverable terminal status, automatically clear the stale lifecycle fields (`status`, `startedAt`, `endedAt`, `runtimeMs`, `abortedLastRun`) while **preserving the `sessionId` and transcript continuity** — no session rotation.

### 2. Clear stale reply operations with race guard (`dispatch-from-config.ts`)

When `admitReplyTurn()` returns `skipped` with reason `active-run` but the persisted session is in terminal status, force-clear the stale reply operation via `forceClearReplyRunBySessionId()` and retry admission **once**. This breaks the deadlock without risking infinite retry loops.

**Concurrent recovery guard**: After a recovery turn clears the proven stale leftover and admits its own fresh operation, it is marked with a `terminalRecovery` flag. If a second visible message arrives against the same terminal snapshot before the first recovery clears the store state, the force-clear branch now skips operations marked as in-flight terminal recoveries. This prevents a sibling recovery turn from killing a fresh recovery that is already in flight, letting concurrent turns fall through to normal busy/wait handling instead.

### 3. Agent API path (`agent.ts`)

Mirror the same terminal recovery logic in the agent API dispatch path so agent API requests also clear terminal state for visible requests.

### 4. Diagnostics (`kernel.ts`, `types.ts`)

Add a structured warning log when a visible channel turn dispatch completes with `queuedFinal: false` and zero tool/block/final counts — a sentinel for future silent message drops. Excludes system turns (heartbeat/cron/exec) and observe-only admissions.

## Real behavior proof

Captured by driving the **real** runtime modules (`dispatchReplyFromConfig`, `initSessionState`, `reply-run-registry`) with verbose logging on, **no vitest and no module mocks** — only the production parameters the Gateway supplies plus a real on-disk JSON session store. A before/after contrast was produced by running the identical driver in a pre-fix worktree.

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A group-chat session left in a terminal lifecycle status (`failed`/`timeout`/`killed`) silently drops every later message — the stale lifecycle entry plus a leftover "active" reply operation block turn admission, so a subsequent visible inbound message gets no AI turn and no reply (#86827).
- **Real environment tested**: Compiled branch source at HEAD `ac52eb0b24`; macOS 15.3.1 (Darwin 24.3.0, arm64), Node `v24.10.0`. Product built with `pnpm build:docker` (exit 0); the recovery log string is present in compiled `dist/`. Evidence captured via `tsx` driving the real in-process dispatch/session/registry path. Before/after contrast run at pre-fix commit `8de37e1ce4` (parent of the first PR commit).
- **Failing proof before fix** (`8de37e1ce4`, identical driver + input): real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible group turn on a `status="failed"` session with a stale running reply op —
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  [dispatch] dispatch TIMED-OUT (no reply within 8s): visible turn stuck behind the stale terminal reply op -> SILENT DROP
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = null
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = true
  ```
  And real `initSessionState()` against an on-disk store: `RESULT: STILL-TERMINAL — session entry remains status=failed after a visible inbound turn`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build:docker
  export OPENCLAW_HOME=$(mktemp -d) OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 \
         OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDERS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CRON=1
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts   # drives real dispatchReplyFromConfig()
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_proof_driver.mts      # drives real initSessionState() + registry
  ```
  The driver seeds a real session store with a terminal `failed` group entry, registers a real stale running reply operation, then calls the real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible inbound group message.
- **Evidence after fix (`ac52eb0b24`)**: copied live console output from running the real in-process driver `node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts` (drives the production `dispatchReplyFromConfig()`); redacted runtime log:
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  dispatch-from-config: cleared stale active reply operation for terminal session agent:main:telegram:group:<redacted>
  [dispatch] dispatch returned: {"queuedFinal":true,"counts":{"tool":0,"block":0,"final":1}}
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = {"kind":"failed","code":"run_failed"}
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = false
  ```
  Real session lifecycle: `initSessionState -> isNewSession=false sessionId=<same> abortedLastRun=false`, and `AFTER turn -> status=undefined ... RESULT: RECOVERED — terminal lifecycle cleared, same sessionId reused (transcript preserved)`.
- **Observed result after fix**: A visible inbound message to a terminal session (1) emits the new recovery log, (2) force-fails the stale leftover op and frees the registry (`isActive=false`), (3) produces a real queued reply (`queuedFinal:true, counts.final:1`) instead of the pre-fix zero-count silent drop / indefinite block, and (4) clears the persisted terminal lifecycle fields while reusing the same `sessionId` (transcript preserved). The pre-fix checkout, given identical input, leaves the session `failed` and the turn stuck/dropped.
- **What was not tested**: The full Docker image build failed at the `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` layer due to transient npm-registry CDN errors fetching optional native packages (network, not OOM and not code), so no end-to-end live channel socket round-trip was performed; the proof drives the real in-process dispatch/session/registry runtime that a channel turn funnels into. The `agent.ts` Gateway RPC path and the `kernel.ts` zero-count warning were source-verified but not separately runtime-exercised here. Concurrency races (sibling visible-turn recovery, heartbeat non-clobber) are covered by the added unit tests. Architecture: arm64 (macOS) only; CI truth is Linux x64 Node 24.

## Tests

- **session.test.ts**: Verify failed group sessions recover without rotating the transcript
- **dispatch-from-config.test.ts**: Verify stale active reply operations are cleared for terminal sessions and admission is retried; verify two concurrent visible turns against the same terminal snapshot do not kill each other's recovery operations
- **agent.test.ts**: Verify terminal failed agent API sessions recover without session rotation; updated existing failed-session tests to verify terminal state clearing
- **kernel.test.ts**: Verify zero-count visible dispatch warning is emitted
- **agent-runner-execution.test.ts / agent-runner-memory.test.ts**: Updated mock `ReplyOperation` stubs with new `terminalRecovery` field and `markTerminalRecovery` method

All focused tests pass across 2 Vitest shards (512 cases).

## Summary

Replaces excessive `as unknown as` type assertions in `src/cron/service/store.ts` with direct type annotations.

`CronStoreFile.jobs` is already typed as `CronJob[]`, so the intermediate cast through `unknown` is unnecessary.

`CronJob` extends `CronJobBase` which is an object type, so it satisfies `Record<string, unknown>` without an intermediate cast.

No behavioral changes — purely removing unsafe type assertions while preserving all validation logic.

## Linked context

Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/91314

---

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Verify that removing redundant `as unknown as` type assertions in `src/cron/service/store.ts` does not break cron store loading, job scheduling, or service status reporting. The cron service must continue to load stored jobs, compute next run times, and report status correctly after the type cleanup.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw instance on Linux x86_64, Node.js v24.16.0, Cron service subsystem, store path `/root/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
1. Applied code changes to `src/cron/service/store.ts` — removed `as unknown as` casts, replaced with direct type annotations.
2. Ran TypeScript compiler: `npx tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json`.
3. Restarted cron service to pick up changes.
4. Executed `openclaw cron status` to verify service loads and reports correctly.
5. Verified cron store file `/root/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json` loads without errors.
6. Ran unit tests: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cron/service/store.test.ts`.

**Evidence after fix:**
```
$ openclaw cron status
{
  "enabled": true,
  "storePath": "/root/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json",
  "jobs": 1,
  "nextWakeAtMs": 1735445760000
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** Cron service status command returns correct output with enabled=true, valid storePath, job count 1, and valid nextWakeAtMs timestamp. No runtime errors thrown. Cron store loads successfully. All original functions work normally. Output is completely consistent with pre-fix behavior. TypeScript compiler accepts the stricter direct types without as unknown as intermediates. All 10 unit tests pass.

**What was not tested:** No manual testing of cron job execution at exact scheduled time. No testing with empty job list or corrupted store file. No testing on Windows or macOS. No load testing with large number of jobs.

---

## Tests and validation

1. Type check: `npx tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json` → No TypeScript errors
2. Unit tests: All cron store test cases passed

---

## Risk checklist

- [x] Did user-visible behavior change? **No** — type-only change, no runtime logic modified
- [x] Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? **No**
- [x] Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? **No**
- [x] Highest-risk area: **None** — compile-time type cleanup only
- [x] Risk mitigation: Type checker passes, all tests pass, runtime behavior verified identical

---

## Current review state

Next action: Ready for maintainer review

target.title: feat(talk): describe_view inline vision for OpenAI Realtime + Gemini Live
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a `describe_view` tool for browser Realtime Talk sessions, letting the
model inspect a single user-approved camera frame during a live voice
conversation. Now covers two providers, both **browser-direct** so the camera
frame is sent straight to the provider and never transits the gateway:

- **OpenAI Realtime (WebRTC)** — frame injected as `input_image` over the
  existing WebRTC data channel.
- **Gemini Live (provider-websocket)** — frame returned inline as
  `functionResponses[].parts[].inlineData` over the existing Live WebSocket.

Still intentionally a Draft PR for product/security review before merge.

## Scope

- Web Control UI only
- Browser-direct transports only: OpenAI Realtime **WebRTC** and Gemini Live
  **provider-websocket**
- User-triggered camera permission flow via a separate Video Talk action
- Browser captures a JPEG frame from the active camera stream only when the
  model calls `describe_view`
- **The gateway registers the tool definition but never receives image data**
  on either path
- `gateway-relay` (which would route frames through the gateway) returns an
  explicit unsupported response and is tracked separately as a
  security-sensitive follow-up in #89131

## Video Talk UX

- Replaced the system Picture-in-Picture preview (which opens a separate OS
  window) with an in-page draggable floating preview widget
- The Talk and Video Talk buttons now each reflect their own start/stop state
  and disable the other mode (previously the Video Talk button disappeared once
  any session was active)

## Security / Privacy Notes

Camera access requires two explicit user actions:

1. The user starts Video Talk instead of regular audio Talk.
2. The user accepts the browser's native camera permission prompt.

If permission is denied, the video talk session does not start. There is no
ambient camera activation. The camera frame is delivered browser→provider
directly on both transports; **no image data passes through the gateway**.

The `Permissions-Policy` change from `camera=()` to `camera=(self)` is required
so the same-origin Control UI can request camera access. The browser permission
prompt remains the actual user consent gate. If maintainers prefer a narrower
policy, for example applying `camera=(self)` only to the Control UI route, I can
adjust the implementation.

## Related Issue

Closes #86425, subject to product/security approval. That issue scoped
`describe_view` to OpenAI WebRTC; this PR additionally covers the Gemini Live
browser-direct path under the same no-gateway-image-data security model. The
gateway-relay generalization (camera frames through the gateway) remains a
separate follow-up in #89131.

target.title: feat(usage): native templated /usage full footer renderer
target.body:
> **Stacks on #89629** (the `usageState` per-turn contract). The first commit here is #89629; review the final commit (`feat(usage): native templated /usage full renderer`). Merge after #89629.

## What
Render the per-reply `/usage full` footer **in core** from a declarative template, instead of requiring a renderer plugin. When `messages.usageTemplate` is set, the footer is produced from it; otherwise the existing built-in line is used unchanged. Fail-open: an absent / unreadable / invalid template, or an empty render, falls back to the built-in line.

```jsonc
"messages": {
  // path (supports ~) …
  "usageTemplate": "~/.openclaw/.usage_bar.json"
  // … or an inline template object
}
```

The template format (`openclaw.usageBar.v1`) is a small declarative DSL: named glyph `scales`, `aliases` (lookup-with-echo), and per-channel `output.surfaces` piece-lists, with verbs `num` / `dur` / `pct` / `inv` / `alias` / `meter` (incl. `meter:1` single glyph and per-window `item_scales`). It is a translator over the contract — no embedded executable; the only external surface is template *data*.

## How
- `src/auto-reply/usage-bar/translator.ts` — the engine. Pure, fail-open, **codepoint-correct** glyph indexing (JS string indexing would split astral emoji).
- `src/auto-reply/usage-bar/contract.ts` — `buildUsageContract`: the per-turn `usageState` snapshot → the `openclaw.usageLine.v1` contract the template reads.
- `src/auto-reply/usage-bar/template.ts` — resolve from a path (mtime-cached) or an inline object.
- `agent-runner.ts` — capture the per-turn snapshot once; at the `/usage full` branch, render the template when configured, else keep the built-in line.
- `messages.usageTemplate` config field + strict zod schema.

No behaviour change unless `messages.usageTemplate` is set.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Customizing the footer today requires a renderer **plugin** that core spawns as a subprocess per reply. This PR renders the footer **in core** from a declarative template (data, not an executable). Opt-in via `messages.usageTemplate`; unset = built-in line unchanged; any error fails open to the built-in line.

**Real environment tested**: MarvinMBP, production OpenClaw `2026.6.1-beta.2` install at `/usr/local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist`. The `usage-footer` **plugin is disabled** in the live config and `messages.usageTemplate` points at the live `~/.openclaw/.usage_bar.json`, so the footer the gateway emits is the in-core path under test.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Loaded the renderer (`translator` / `contract` / `template`) and rendered the live template against a representative per-turn snapshot for both surfaces, then exercised the fail-open paths — no plugin, no subprocess:
```console
$ node --import tsx render-proof.mts     # loadUsageBarTemplate("~/.openclaw/.usage_bar.json") -> buildUsageContract(snapshot, surface) -> renderUsageBar(...)
$ grep -rl 'function renderUsageBar' /usr/local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist
$ jq '.plugins.entries["usage-footer"].enabled, .messages.usageTemplate' ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
```

**Evidence after fix**: console output against the live install + live template:
```console
template loaded from live path: true

[discord] core-rendered footer:
-# -
-# 🩺 gpt5.5✏️ | med🐌 | 📚 [⣿⣿⣿⣿⣷]272k | ↕ 10k/21🗄 94%📊 🌤4h45m 🍀6.6d

[telegram] core-rendered footer:
—
gpt5.5 ✏️ med 🐌 |[⣿⣿⣿⣿⣷]272k
📊 🌤4h45m 🍀6.6d

# fail-open (renderUsageBar never throws; "" -> core's built-in line):
null template                -> ""
non-object template          -> ""
throwing getter in pieces    -> ""
empty object                 -> ""
loadUsageBarTemplate({})     -> undefined   (custom line skipped entirely)
```
Live install confirms this is the in-core path, not the plugin: `grep -rl 'function renderUsageBar' .../dist` -> `dist/agent-runner.runtime-Djok-Gt3.js`; `usage-footer` plugin `enabled = false`; `messages.usageTemplate = ~/.openclaw/.usage_bar.json`.

**Observed result after fix**: Core turns the user-supplied template **data** into the exact multi-line custom footer for both Discord and Telegram (aliases `gpt-5.5`->`gpt5.5`/`medium`->`med`, override `✏️`, braille context meter, per-window `weather`/`plants` limit glyphs) with the renderer plugin disabled. Malformed, throwing, or empty templates render `""` and core falls back to its built-in line; an unset `usageTemplate` skips the custom path. A footer **screenshot is intentionally not the proof**: it cannot show *which* code rendered the line — the disabled plugin + the shipped `renderUsageBar` reproducing the string byte-for-byte can.

**What was not tested**: Live per-surface delivery fan-out beyond Discord/Telegram. Known sharp edge: an *unknown verb* degrades to literal text (e.g. `bogusverb:::`) rather than dropping the segment — graceful and non-throwing, but not silent; tightening that is a follow-up. Unit tests (`usage-bar/translator.test.ts`: verb parity, all segment forms, astral-glyph correctness) and `run-tsgo` clean / schema `--check` ok are supplemental, not the proof.

## Why
Lets an operator fully customize the usage footer (per channel, multi-line, glyphs, aliases) with config + a data template, with zero runtime cost when unused and no plugin/subprocess. Pairs with #89629 (the data) and the optional persistent-`/usage` default (#89762).

target.title: fix(ui): default workspace files rail to hidden, add toggle button
target.body:
## Summary

- Hides the "Workspace Files" side rail by default in the chat UI
- Adds a toggle button in the composer toolbar to show/hide the rail
- Fixes a regression introduced in v2026.6.1 where the rail was always visible, compressing the chat area and reducing readability on desktop

**Changes (6 files, +45/-3 lines):**

1. **CSS** (`sidebar.css`): `.chat-workbench` defaults to single column; `.chat-workbench--with-rail` restores the two-column layout
2. **Chat props** (`chat.ts`): Added `workspaceFileRailVisible` and `onToggleWorkspaceFileRail` props
3. **Toggle button** (`chat.ts`): Placed in the composer toolbar alongside existing input buttons, uses `agent-chat__input-btn--active` style when visible
4. **App state** (`app-view-state.ts`): Added `workspaceFileRailVisible: boolean`
5. **App component** (`app.ts`): Added `@state() workspaceFileRailVisible = false` and handler
6. **Render wiring** (`app-render.ts`): Passes state and toggle handler to chat component

## Linked context

Closes #90359

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: After upgrading to v2026.6.1, the Workspace Files side rail is shown by default on desktop, compressing the chat area and reducing readability
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw instance (Linux x86_64, Node v24, Playwright Chromium headless)
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  # UI build — passes
  pnpm run build
  # ✓ built in 1.47s

  # UI test suite — 94 tests, all passed
  npx vitest run ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts
  # Test Files  1 passed (1)
  #       Tests  94 passed (94)
  ```
- Evidence after fix:

  ![Before vs After](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/92a755d4-7492-4f00-a355-1a316dc41df5)

- Observed result after fix: Workspace Files rail is hidden by default, restoring full-width chat. A toggle button in the composer toolbar allows users to show files when needed
- What was not tested: End-to-end screenshot of the actual WebChat UI (requires a running Gateway with connected state); the fix is validated by the UI test suite
- Proof limitations: The before/after screenshot is a simulated layout equivalent to the CSS grid change; the actual fix is verified by 94 passing UI tests

## Tests and validation

- `npx vitest run ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` — 94 tests (all passing)
- `pnpm run build` — builds successfully

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` — Workspace Files rail is now hidden by default; users must click the "Files" button to show it

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

What is the highest-risk area? Users who relied on the rail being always visible may need to click a button to access workspace files. The fix preserves full access through an explicit toggle.

How is that risk mitigated? The toggle button uses the same visual style and placement as other composer toolbar buttons. The rail state resets on page reload (default hidden), matching the pattern of other optional UI panels.

## Current review state

What is the next action? Ready for maintainer review.

target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the official `qwen3.7-plus` tiered pricing metadata to the bundled `opencode-go` catalog.
- Keeps the existing flat first-tier prices as the fallback/default row values, while adding the `>256K` tier used by cost estimation paths that understand `tieredPricing`.
- Adds focused coverage for the opencode-go catalog row and the high-context cost estimator behavior.
- Intentionally does not change provider routing, live catalog discovery, auth, or inference transport behavior.

## Linked context

Closes #91238

Related #91239

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Not directly. This follows the live issue and the ClawSweeper review on #91239, which identified missing `qwen3.7-plus` tiered pricing as the remaining merge risk.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `opencode-go/qwen3.7-plus` no longer underestimates high-context usage-cost summaries by using only the `<=256K` flat pricing row while advertising a 1M context window.
- Real environment tested: macOS arm64 worktree from `upstream/main` at `538d36eaaa`, Node v24.15.0, pnpm v11.2.2, local OpenClaw source checkout.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1/models | node -e 'let s="";process.stdin.on("data",d=>s+=d);process.stdin.on("end",()=>{const j=JSON.parse(s); const ids=(j.data||j.models||[]).map(m=>m.id||m.name).filter(id=>["minimax-m3","qwen3.7-max","qwen3.7-plus"].includes(id)); console.log(ids.join("\\n"));})'
node --import tsx - <<'EOF_NODE'
import { registerSingleProviderPlugin } from 'openclaw/plugin-sdk/plugin-test-runtime';
import { estimateUsageCost } from './src/utils/usage-format.ts';
import plugin from './extensions/opencode-go/index.ts';
const provider = await registerSingleProviderPlugin(plugin);
const model = provider.resolveDynamicModel?.({ modelId: 'qwen3.7-plus' });
const usage = { input: 300_000, output: 1_000, cacheRead: 10_000, cacheWrite: 2_000 };
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  model: model?.provider + '/' + model?.id,
  contextWindow: model?.contextWindow,
  flatCost: model?.cost && estimateUsageCost({ usage, cost: { input: model.cost.input, output: model.cost.output, cacheRead: model.cost.cacheRead, cacheWrite: model.cost.cacheWrite } }),
  tieredCost: estimateUsageCost({ usage, cost: model?.cost }),
  tieredPricing: model?.cost.tieredPricing,
}, null, 2));
EOF_NODE
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts src/utils/usage-format.test.ts
pnpm test:extension opencode-go
git diff --check
```

```text

  "model": "opencode-go/qwen3.7-plus",

    { "input": 0.4, "output": 1.6, "cacheRead": 0.04, "cacheWrite": 0.5, "range": [0, 256000] },
    { "input": 1.2, "output": 4.8, "cacheRead": 0.12, "cacheWrite": 1.5, "range": [256000] }
  ]
}

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts src/utils/usage-format.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1); Tests 33 passed (33)
Test Files  1 passed (1); Tests 11 passed (11)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards

pnpm test:extension opencode-go
Test Files  4 passed (4); Tests 15 passed (15)

git diff --check
(no output)
```

- Observed result after fix: the opencode-go runtime catalog resolves `qwen3.7-plus` with the official two-tier pricing metadata, and `estimateUsageCost` selects the high-context tier for a 300K-input usage sample, producing `$0.369` instead of the flat first-tier `$0.123`.
- What was not tested: authenticated live inference through OpenCode Go and a real gateway `models list` run. The local gateway on this machine required pairing/scope approval, so I did not use it as proof.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this proof validates the provider catalog/runtime registration path and usage-cost estimator behavior from source. It does not claim to change transport-time flat cost calculation; session usage aggregation already recomputes tiered pricing when configured.
- Before evidence: current `upstream/main` already includes the new model IDs, but `qwen3.7-plus` only carried the flat `<=256K` rates, so the same 300K-input sample would estimate `$0.123` from flat metadata instead of `$0.369` from the official high-context tier.

target.title: fix(memory): resolve embedding provider by authProviderId fallback
target.body:
## Summary

- When a user configures `memorySearch.provider` to `"google"`, the embedding provider lookup failed with "Unknown memory embedding provider: google" because the registered adapter id is `"gemini"` while the config-level provider name is `"google"`.
- The gemini memory embedding adapter (`extensions/google/memory-embedding-adapter.ts`) declares `authProviderId: "google"`.
- This fix adds an `authProviderId` fallback once in `getMemoryEmbeddingProvider`, the shared runtime resolver in `src/plugins/memory-embedding-provider-runtime.ts`, so all consumers (memory manager, Gateway `/v1/embeddings`, startup warnings, doctor checks) agree on the resolution.
- No duplicated call-site scans — the fallback lives in the single shared resolver.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts` → 8 passed (includes regression test for "google" → "gemini")
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts` → 36 passed
- Real production-path proof: registered the actual gemini adapter and verified resolution (see below)

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix / [ ] Feature / [x] Refactor / [ ] Docs / [ ] Security / [ ] Chore

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / [ ] Skills / [ ] Auth / [x] Memory / [ ] Integrations / [ ] API / [ ] UI/UX / [ ] CI

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `memorySearch.provider: "google"` caused "Unknown memory embedding provider: google" in `--index` and `--deep` because the gemini adapter id ("gemini") did not match the config-level provider name ("google"). The fix adds a centralized `authProviderId` fallback so "google" resolves to the registered gemini adapter.

**Environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, `node --import tsx` calling the actual production `registerMemoryEmbeddingProvider` + `getMemoryEmbeddingProvider` from the fix branch with the real gemini adapter from `extensions/google/memory-embedding-adapter.ts`.

**Steps run after the patch:** Registered the production gemini adapter (mirroring what the Google plugin does at gateway startup), then called `getMemoryEmbeddingProvider("google")` — the exact code path that `--index` and `--deep` hit when `memorySearch.provider` is "google".

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
Registered gemini adapter:
  id: gemini
  authProviderId: google
  defaultModel: gemini-embedding-001

=== Reproducing #90786: memorySearch.provider = "google" ===

After fix: "google" resolves to adapter "gemini"
  -> This is the fix. Before, this returned undefined and threw:
     "Unknown memory embedding provider: google"

"gemini" direct lookup: OK

"nonexistent" lookup: undefined (correct)
```

**Observed result after the fix:** `getMemoryEmbeddingProvider("google")` correctly resolves to the gemini adapter via the centralized `authProviderId` fallback. Direct lookup by adapter id ("gemini") continues to work. Unknown providers return `undefined` with no false positives.

**Not tested:** Live Rocky Linux gateway with real Google AI Studio API key, full `openclaw memory status --index --agent main` run, and memory index rebuild.

Closes #90786

target.title: [Bug]: Active memory injection breaks prompt cache hit rate (99.9% → 22%)
target.body:
[Bug]: Active memory injection breaks prompt cache hit rate (99.9% → 22%)

## Phenomenon
Enabling the `active-memory` plugin causes prompt cache hit rate to collapse
from ~99.9% (clean baseline) to ~22% in production dashboard observations.

Reproduction across two Anthropic-compatible providers (independent test runs
by two coding agents) shows:
- Clean baseline (5 identical prompts, no prependContext): 99.9% warm hit
- Static 32KB prependContext (5 identical prompts, fixed content): 99.8% warm hit
- Variable 32KB prependContext (5 prompts, content changes per call): 0.0% warm hit

## Root cause
The `active-memory` plugin injects recalled memories via the `before_prompt_build`
hook, returning content through `hookResult.prependContext`. In the prompt-preparation
layer, this context is concatenated **into the user message** (not the system prompt).

For every eligible conversational reply, the plugin spawns a blocking memory
sub-agent that runs `memory_search` to recall facts. The recall query is derived
from the current user message, so different user messages → different recalled
fact lists → character-level changes in `prependContext`.

Anthropic protocol `cache_control: {type: "ephemeral"}` markers are intended to
isolate the stable system-prompt block from the variable user-message block.
However, the observed behavior across two Anthropic-compatible providers is that
the cache_control boundary does not work as expected: any character-level change
in the user-message block causes the entire prompt to miss cache (0% hit rate),
instead of just the variable part.

## Reproduction
1. Configure any Anthropic-compatible provider
2. Enable `active-memory` with `config.agents: ["main"]`
3. Send 5 near-identical user messages (e.g. "hi count 3")
4. With prependContext: 32KB of varied content per call, observe:
   - cache_creation_input_tokens and cache_read_input_tokens both = 0
   - All 5 calls rebuild the entire prompt (warm hit rate = 0%)
5. Production dashboard: 22% (weighted average across triggered and non-triggered
   conversational turns)

## Code-level trace

### prependContext injection site
In the prompt preparation layer, `hookResult.prependContext` is concatenated
directly before the user prompt:
```
// selection-DrXxngyT.js ~L12796
effectivePrompt = `${hookResult.prependContext}\n\n${effectivePrompt}`;
```

### active-memory hook returns prependContext
```
// active-memory/index.js ~L1743: before_prompt_build hook
// ~L1829: return value
return { prependContext: promptPrefix };
// promptPrefix = "Untrusted context..." + XML-wrapped summary
```

### cache_control placement
```
// anthropic-payload-policy-jufdNb_5.js ~L66-85
// cache_control placed on the LAST block of the LAST user message
// This should isolate the stable prefix from the variable suffix,
// but does not on the tested endpoints.
```

### memory_search tool registration
```
// memory-core/index.js ~L270
api.registerTool(..., { names: ["memory_search"] });
// Semantic search tool: different queries → different results
```

## Suggested fixes
1. **Document the limitation**: At minimum, document that enabling `active-memory`
   effectively disables prompt cache for any user message that includes variable
   recalled content. This is silent and undocumented.

2. **Stabilize prependContext output**: Have the active-memory plugin produce
   deterministic output for a given session (e.g. fixed ordering, fixed truncation,
   hash-based fingerprint in place of timestamps) so character-level changes don't
   propagate to the user-message block.

3. **Skip active-memory for cache-critical prompts**: Allow callers to mark
   "cache-critical" prefixes that bypass active-memory injection.

4. **Tighten cache_control boundary**: Make the Anthropic cache boundary respect
   the prependContext vs system-prompt split so that stable system-prompt content
   is cached independently of variable user-message content.

## Workaround
Disable `active-memory` (set `enabled: false`) and use `memory_search` explicitly
when needed. Cache hit rate returns to ~99.9%.

## Impact
Any user with `active-memory` enabled and an Anthropic-compatible provider that
supports prompt caching will see cache hit rate collapse. This is silent (no
warning, no log entry) and undocumented. Common configuration, common failure mode.

target.title: fix(tts): strip reasoning tags from summarized text before speech
target.body:
## Summary

- When a reasoning model (e.g., MiniMax with `reasoning: true`) is used as the TTS summary model, its output can include `<think>` / `<thinking>` / `<thought>` tags wrapping chain-of-thought content.
- These tags were passed through to the speech synthesis engine, producing unintelligible audio instead of a clean summary.
- This fix strips reasoning tags from the assembled summary text in `summarizeText` before returning it, reusing the existing `stripReasoningTagsFromText` helper that already handles nested tags, code regions, and orphan close boundaries.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/tts/tts-core.test.ts` → 2 passed (including new regression test)
- Production `summarizeText` verified via `node --import tsx` with real mocks (see Real behavior proof below)

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix / [ ] Feature / [ ] Refactor / [ ] Docs / [ ] Security / [ ] Chore

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / [ ] Skills / [ ] Auth / [ ] Memory / [ ] Integrations / [ ] API / [x] UI/UX / [ ] CI

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** When a reasoning model is used for TTS summarization, the model output includes `<think>` / `<thinking>` / `<thought>` tags that wrap chain-of-thought content. These tags reach the speech synthesis engine, producing garbled audio instead of a clean summary.

**Environment tested:** Node 24.x on Linux, `node --import tsx` calling the actual patched `summarizeText` production function from the fix branch with mocked dependencies that simulate a reasoning model.

**Steps run after the patch:** Called `summarizeText` from source via `node --import tsx` with:
1. A mock `completeSimple` that returns a reasoning-model-style response containing `<think>...</think>` followed by clean summary text
2. A mock `completeSimple` that returns plain summary text without thinking tags (passthrough control)

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
=== Real behavior proof: summarizeText with reasoning model ===

Test 1: Reasoning model with <think> tags
  Input chars: 740
  Summary: Today will be sunny with a high of 75 degrees Fahrenheit and light winds from the southwest.
  Contains <think>: false
  Expected: "Today will be sunny with a high of 75 degrees Fahrenheit and light winds from the southwest."
  Match: true

Test 2: Clean model without think tags (passthrough)
  Summary: The weather is clear and warm.
  Contains <think>: false
  Match: true

All tests passed: summarizeText correctly strips reasoning tags before returning summary.
```

**Observed result after the fix:** The patched `summarizeText` function correctly strips `<think>` tags from reasoning model output before returning the summary, while clean text without thinking tags passes through unmodified. The function produces output suitable for TTS audio synthesis.

**Not tested:** Live TTS audio flow with a real Microsoft/MiniMax TTS provider and an actual reasoning summary model generating spoken audio output.

Closes #90364

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches
